title: King's Edge deliverable measure schema
description: Authoring schema for deliverable-level measures. Prefer benefit-level measures when a measure tests one benefit. Use deliverable-level measures for cross-cutting indicators and map them to benefits with supportsBenefits.

measureType.description: Type of evidence, for example usage, qualitative feedback, judgement-based review, adoption, quality assurance, readiness or quantitative indicator.

questionAnswered.description: The evidence question this measure answers. It should usually be phrased around benefit realisation.

measure.description: How evidence will be gathered or interpreted. This should test value, not just activity completion.

supportsBenefits.description: Benefit IDs supported by this measure. Required in practice for cross-cutting deliverable-level measures.

examples.title: Programme story and responsible interpretation quality
examples.questionAnswered: Are colleagues using the evidence to tell accurate, context-sensitive programme stories and interpret caveats responsibly?
examples.measure: Review of early profiles, caveat use, facilitator feedback and programme confirmation of story material.
